task_completion.description: Load the specified pre-recorded motor imagery data file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI system control panel or playback settings show 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt' selected as the active playback file.

task_completion.description: Set up the EMG widget in the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'EMG' widget is open and visible within the OpenBCI GUI layout.

task_completion.description: Isolate Channel 1 in the EMG widget.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the EMG widget, Channel 1 is toggled ON (typically indicated by a colored indicator, active button, or visible signal graph for Chan 1).

task_completion.description: Disable Channels 2 through 8 in the EMG widget.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the EMG widget, Channels 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 are toggled OFF (typically indicated by grayed-out buttons, inactive indicators, or removed signal graphs).

task_completion.description: Adjust the EMG threshold above the noise floor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The threshold setting (slider, horizontal line, or numerical value) for Channel 1 in the EMG widget is visibly adjusted to sit above the baseline noise level of the resting signal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is visible and actively being used, rather than terminal commands or scripts executing the configuration.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just type the expected output into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen does not show a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) containing the task description or manually typed configuration files to fake completion.

integrity.description: Verify the settings are applied within the actual software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The EMG widget, channel toggles, and threshold adjustments are genuinely displayed and modified within the OpenBCI GUI interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct recording file to load is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt'. The target widget is the 'EMG' widget. Channel 1 must be the only active channel in the EMG widget, while channels 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 must be disabled.
